title: Trail Blazers Thank You — Adrianna gratitude email draft
html:
TL;DR

  • Dec 31, 2023 gratitude email draft from Yahir to Adrianna, thanking her for hosting a Trail Blazers game experience for him and his family.
  • Two versions in the doc — a casual first draft ("unbelievably curdiosuty and abundantly kind") and a polished second version ("Your generosity and care in planning such a thoughtful event was so appreciated").
  • Likely the same scholarship-comped Blazers game referenced in the 2023 year recap December bullet ("went to a full-fledged escorted blazers game bc of scholarships").
  • Adrianna is presumably a Ford Family Foundation / Trail Blazers community-relations contact — fits the debt of gratitude email genre Yahir runs whenever a scholarship-funded experience lands.
  • A thank-you note for an experience that landed in the year recap as a top moment the next day — December 31 + January 1, written back-to-back.

Notes

Pure debt of gratitude artifact. The two-version drafting move is canonical Yahir — he writes the emotional draft first ("endless thanks," "unbelievably curdiosuty") then a more measured second version. Both are preserved in the doc. He likely sent the second.

Adrianna is a single-mention contact at this point — does not warrant a stub yet. Logging to Potential Entities: Adrianna (Trail Blazers / Ford-orbit community contact, 2023). Promote on next mention.

The juxtaposition with the year recap written the next day is significant — the Blazers game was emotionally important enough to make the year-end best-of, AND important enough to merit a same-week follow-up thank-you. That's the debt of gratitude reflex working: the experience isn't complete until the gratitude is sent.

Connections

  • debt of gratitude — the rhetorical / emotional frame
  • 2024 01 01 2023 year recap — the next-day artifact that confirms this experience's weight
  • ford family foundation · trail blazers — likely sponsor / venue
  • — the relationship genre

Privacy

  • "Adrianna" first name only — retained, not redacted (no last name, no contact info, no organizational identifier).
